ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific Parmigiana-style pizza code exists; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole 12-inch Pizza alla Parmigiana, approximately 500 g, typically served as a single-person meal in an Italian restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with vegetables, NS as to type of crust (FDC ID 2097512) and component ingredient data for eggplant, mozzarella, Parmesan, tomato sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Pizza alla Parmigiana exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central vegetable pizza entries (FDC ID 2097512, 'Pizza with vegetables, NS as to type of crust') combined with component-level data for baked eggplant, buffalo mozzarella, Parmesan cheese, and tomato sauce. The dish is a thin-crust Italian pizza topped with baked eggplant, tomato sauce, buffalo mozzarella, Parmesan, and basil. Nutrient values represent a weighted composite estimate per 100 g of the assembled, baked pizza. Energy and macros are consistent with published values for vegetable-topped Italian-style pizza in the USDA database.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Pizza alla Parmigiana was found. GI of 50 is estimated based on published GI values for thin-crust cheese-and-vegetable pizza in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which report GI values for pizza in the range of 45–60 depending on crust thickness and toppings. A value of 50 is used as a conservative central estimate for a thin-crust Italian pizza with vegetable toppings, consistent with the low-GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 132.5) / 100 = 66.3, where carbohydrate per 500 g serving = 26.5 × 5 = 132.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2400 mg per whole pizza)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this pizza due to its high sodium content from cheese and tomato sauce; a single 12-inch pizza can provide approximately 2400 mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) and high glycemic load per whole pizza serving (~66)
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the GI of this pizza is in the low range (~50), the large serving size (500 g whole pizza) results in a high glycemic load (~66 per serving), so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion (1–2 slices) and pair with a salad to moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from buffalo mozzarella and Parmesan cheese (~4.1 g per 100 g) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content from buffalo mozzarella and Parmesan cheese, combined with high sodium, warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease; portion control to 2–3 slices and choosing lower-sodium cheese alternatives where possible is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza alla Parmigiana is a vegetarian Italian pizza inspired by the classic Sicilian and Southern Italian dish Melanzane alla Parmigiana (eggplant Parmesan). A 12-inch version, as served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore, features a thin Italian-style pizza base topped with baked or grilled eggplant (aubergine) slices, rich tomato sauce, creamy buffalo mozzarella, finely grated Parmesan cheese, and fresh basil leaves, then baked in a wood-fired or deck oven until the crust is golden and the cheese is melted and lightly browned. The flavour profile is savoury and umami-rich, with the sweetness of tomato sauce balanced by the mild bitterness of eggplant, the creaminess of buffalo mozzarella, the sharp saltiness of Parmesan, and the aromatic freshness of basil. The texture combines a crisp, chewy crust with soft, yielding eggplant and molten cheese. Nutritionally, per 100 g the pizza provides approximately 218 kcal, 9.2 g protein, 8.5 g fat (of which 4.1 g saturated), and 26.5 g carbohydrate, with 2.2 g dietary fibre and 480 mg sodium. The dish is a good source of calcium from the two cheeses. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low GI), though the glycemic load per whole pizza is high (~66) due to the large serving size. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for hypertensive patients, and the saturated fat from dairy cheeses warrants moderation for cardiovascular health. The dish is suitable for lacto-vegetarians but contains gluten and dairy. In Singapore, this pizza is available at Italian restaurants such as Ricciotti and is priced at approximately SGD 28.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a 12-inch Pizza alla Parmigiana?
What toppings are on Pizza alla Parmigiana?
What is the GI of eggplant pizza?
Can diabetics eat Pizza alla Parmigiana?
How much sodium is in Pizza alla Parmigiana?
What is the difference between Pizza alla Parmigiana and Melanzane alla Parmigiana?
Where can I find Pizza alla Parmigiana in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No exact match for Pizza alla Parmigiana was found in any primary approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central exact entry). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central vegetable pizza entries and component-level ingredient data. GI is estimated from analogous thin-crust vegetable pizza values in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Confidence is medium due to the adapted/estimated nature of both nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA FoodData Central vegetable pizza data (FDC ID 2097512) and component ingredient data; no exact database entry for Pizza alla Parmigiana exists in approved sources. 2. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate from published thin-crust vegetable pizza GI values; direct measurement for this specific dish is not available. 3. Serving size of 500 g is an estimate for a whole 12-inch Italian-style pizza; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA vegetable pizza source entry and is not reliably estimated from component data. 5. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record is incorrect for this vegetarian pizza and has been removed. 6. The dish is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk, Singapore, at SGD 28.34. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; Ricciotti is not listed as a halal-certified restaurant. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 50 × carbohydrate per serving 132.5 g) / 100 = 66.3, where carbohydrate per 500 g serving = 26.5 g/100g × 500 g = 132.5 g.
